IIT Delhi
ELL7282/ELL782
Semester I, 2026-27

  • Home
  • Schedule
  • Lectures

  • Tutorials
  • Quizzes
  • Resources

Lectures

Tentative Topics

  • 1. Foundations of Computer Architecture
    • Software–Hardware Interface
      • Relationship between software and hardware
      • Program execution on hardware
      • Role of instruction sets
    • Instruction Set Architecture
      • Instruction set architecture concepts
      • SimpleRisc ISA
      • Load–store architecture
      • Instruction categories
  • 2. Data Representation
    • Digital Information
      • Bits and binary logic
      • Boolean algebra
      • Binary number systems
    • Integer Representation
      • Unsigned and signed integers
      • Integer representation formats
      • Integer overflow
    • Floating-Point Representation
      • Floating-point formats
      • Range and precision
      • Floating-point overflow
    • Character Representation
      • Character encodings
      • Binary representation of text
  • 3. Assembly Language and Machine Instructions
    • Assembly-Language Fundamentals
      • Registers and register organization
      • Memory and memory organization
      • Assembly instruction syntax
    • Arithmetic and Logical Operations
      • Arithmetic instructions
      • Logical instructions
      • Register operations
      • Immediate operands and modifiers
    • Memory-Access Operations
      • Load and store instructions
      • Addressing modes
      • Register–memory interaction
    • Control Flow
      • Branch instructions
      • Conditional execution
      • Loops
      • Control structures in assembly
    • Functions and Procedures
      • Function calls and returns
      • Argument passing and return values
      • Register spilling
      • Stack organization
      • Activation records
    • Instruction Encoding
      • Opcodes and instruction fields
      • Register, immediate, and branch formats
      • 32-bit machine-code representation
  • 4. Digital Logic
    • Transistor and CMOS Foundations
      • Transistors as switches
      • CMOS logic
      • Inverters, NAND gates, and NOR gates
    • Combinational Logic
      • Multiplexers and demultiplexers
      • Decoders and encoders
    • Sequential Logic
      • State in digital circuits
      • Clock signals and clocked circuits
      • State storage and transfer
    • Latches and Flip-Flops
      • SR and clocked latches
      • D and J-K flip-flops
      • Edge-triggered operation
  • 5. Computer Arithmetic
    • Basic Adders
      • Half adders
      • Full adders
    • Ripple-Carry Addition
      • Ripple-carry adders
      • Carry propagation
      • Delay analysis
    • Fast Addition
      • Carry-select adders
      • Carry-lookahead adders
      • Generate and propagate logic
      • Parallel and hierarchical carry computation
  • 6. Memory Circuit Design
    • SRAM
      • SRAM cells and arrays
      • SRAM read and write operations
    • DRAM
      • DRAM cells and arrays
      • DRAM read and write operations
      • Precharging and sensing
      • DRAM refresh
    • Content-Addressable Memory
      • CAM organization
      • Associative lookup
      • CAM arrays
  • 7. Processor Design
    • Processor Stages
      • Instruction Fetch (IF)
      • Operand Fetch (OF)
      • Execute (EX)
      • Memory Access (MA)
      • Register Write-Back (RW)
    • Processor Datapath
      • Register file
      • Arithmetic logic unit
      • Instruction decoding
      • Memory-access path
      • Branch datapath
      • Data movement between stages
    • Processor Control Path
      • Control-signal generation
      • Datapath coordination
      • Stage-specific control
  • 8. Control-Unit Design
    • Hardwired Control
      • Opcode and instruction-bit decoding
      • Combinational control logic
      • Control-signal generation
    • Microprogrammed Control
      • Microinstructions and microprograms
      • Microcontrol unit
      • Internal registers and shared datapath
    • Microassembly and Microcode
      • Microassembly language
      • Microinstruction control flow
      • Microcode for ALU, load/store, branch, call, and return instructions
    • Microcontrol-Unit Organization
      • Microprogram counter
      • Microinstruction memory and sequencing
      • Horizontal and vertical microprogramming
      • Microinstruction encoding
  • 9. Instruction Pipelining
    • Pipeline Fundamentals
      • Overlapping instruction execution
      • Pipeline stages and registers
      • Pipeline throughput
    • Pipeline Hazards
      • Data and read-after-write hazards
      • Control hazards
      • Structural hazards
    • Software Hazard Handling
      • NOP insertion
      • Pipeline stalls
      • Instruction reordering
    • Hardware Hazard Handling
      • Pipeline interlocks
      • Stall and bubble insertion
      • Data forwarding and bypassing
    • Advanced Pipeline Issues
      • Load-use hazards
      • Branch penalties
      • Hazards not eliminated by forwarding
    • Pipeline Performance
      • Cycles per instruction and instructions per cycle
      • Clock frequency and stall rate
      • Pipeline depth
      • Pipeline depth–frequency trade-off
  • 10. Memory Hierarchy
    • Memory-System Trade-Offs
      • Latency and capacity
      • Area and power consumption
      • Cost and performance trade-offs
    • Locality
      • Temporal locality
      • Spatial locality
    • Cache Fundamentals
      • Cache hierarchy and cache blocks
      • Cache hits and misses
      • Cache lookup
    • Cache Organizations
      • Direct-mapped caches
      • Fully associative caches
      • Set-associative caches
    • Cache Read and Write Policies
      • Cache read and write operations
      • Write-through policy
      • Write-back policy
    • Cache Replacement Policies
      • Least Recently Used (LRU)
      • Pseudo-LRU
      • Replacement-candidate selection
    • Cache Performance
      • Hit rate and miss rate
      • Miss penalty
      • Average Memory Access Time (AMAT)
  • 11. Virtual Memory
    • Address Spaces
      • Virtual and physical addresses
      • Virtual address space and physical memory
    • Address Translation
      • Virtual-to-physical translation
      • Page tables
    • Paging
      • Pages
      • Page-based memory management
      • Efficient use of physical memory

Laboratory for Computational Social Systems (LCS2)
Indian Institute of Technology Delhi
Hauz Khas, New Delhi - 110016, India
